name: Story of the World
description: Engaging narrative world history in 4 chronological volumes with activity books, maps, coloring pages, and audiobook options.
notes: The most popular elementary homeschool history curriculum. Four volumes: Ancient Times, Middle Ages, Early Modern Times, Modern Age. Faith-friendly (covers Biblical events as history) but not faith-based — treats all religions evenhandedly. Activity books are optional and extensive — choose what fits your schedule. Available in print, digital, and audiobook. Best for grades 1-4 as read-aloud; 5-8 can use independently with activity book. Volume 4 has fewer hands-on activities.

name: The Mystery of History
description: Chronological world history from a biblical worldview in 4 volumes with multi-age activities, timeline, and mapwork for K-12.
notes: Classical, chronological, Christian approach. Activities divided into younger/middle/older age ranges for multi-age family use. 28-week curriculum, 2-3 lessons per week. Companion Guide includes mapwork, timelines, assessments, and book lists. Vol 1: Creation to Resurrection, Vol 2: Early Church to Middle Ages, Vol 3: Renaissance to Growth of Nations, Vol 4: Wars of Independence to Modern Times. High schoolers need supplemental reading and a separate US History course. Can feel dry/fact-intensive for very young students (K-3).

name: Beautiful Feet Books
description: Charlotte Mason-inspired, literature-based history using living books with teacher guides, narration, notebooking, and timeline activities.
notes: Founded 1984. Literature packs include teacher guide + 10-20 living books per course. Lessons organized by topic (not 5-day weeks) for flexibility. Heavy on narration (oral and written), notebooking, and map/timeline work. Christian company but curriculum could be adapted for secular use. Can cover history + geography + writing + art in early grades. No worksheets or tests. High school Modern History counts as 2 credits (History + Language Arts). Can use library books to reduce cost.

name: Notgrass History
description: Visually rich, photo-heavy Christian history curriculum integrating Bible study, language arts, and hands-on projects for grades 1-12.
notes: Three tiers: Elementary (grades 1-4), Middle School (5-8), High School (9-12). Includes Bible study and language arts assignments within history lessons. Photo-heavy and vibrant — great for visual learners. No separate teacher's guide needed — all instructions included. Middle/high school students can work independently. Titles: Our Star-Spangled Story, Our 50 States, From Adam to Us, America the Beautiful, Exploring America, Exploring World History, etc. Covers US history, world history, geography, government, and economics.

name: Tapestry of Grace
description: Comprehensive classical Christian unit-study curriculum using history as the organizing theme for literature, writing, fine arts, geography, and Bible across K-12.
notes: Award-winning eclectic curriculum blending Classical, Charlotte Mason, unit studies, and lapbooking. Four classical learning levels: lower grammar (K-3), upper grammar (3-6), dialectic (6-9), rhetoric (9-12). Four-year cycle through world history — all ages study same period simultaneously. Covers history, writing, literature, fine arts, geography, church history, Bible. Does NOT include math, science, phonics, foreign language. Heavy parent prep (1-2 hours/week pre-reading). Weekly plans ~50 pages. Best value for multi-age families planning to use through high school. Free 3-week sample available.

name: History Quest
description: Secular, story-format world history read-aloud with optional study guides covering ancient through modern times.
notes: From the makers of Real Science Odyssey. Chapter-book format designed as family read-aloud (grades 1-4) or independent read (5+). Volumes: Early Times, Middle Times, Modern Times, plus US History & Civics. Study guides sold separately with activities, maps, and timeline work. History Odyssey bridge series for grades 6-12. Neutral on religion — presents events based on historical facts. 3 weeks of free 'try before you buy.' Very budget-friendly. Same publisher as RSO — pairs well for secular families.

name: BookShark History
description: Secular, literature-based history curriculum using diverse living books, historical fiction, and biographies with fully planned 4-day schedules.
notes: Secular spin-off of Sonlight. Levels A-J (PreK through 10th grade). History & Literature integrated — students read ~50 books/year. Level F (Eastern history) is especially beloved. Religiously neutral — covers faith's historical role from multiple perspectives. 36-week, 4-day schedule with detailed instructor's guide. Includes read-alouds, independent readers, timeline and map resources. Reusable across siblings. Can pair with BookShark Science for full secular package. Virtual option available for online tracking.

name: Sonlight History
description: Christian literature-based history using living books, historical fiction, and read-alouds with fully planned daily schedules and timeline resources.
notes: Originally designed for missionary families overseas. History/Bible/Literature integrated into one package. Christian perspective but explores world religions and cultures. Each level spans 3-year age range for multi-age teaching. 4-day or 5-day schedule options. Extensive reading load — requires dedicated parent read-aloud time. Expensive but books are reusable. Save 15-20% with All-Subjects Package. Payment plans available.

name: Simply Charlotte Mason History
description: Charlotte Mason-method history using a 6-year cycle, living books, narration, and nature study for the whole family grades 1-12.
notes: Pure Charlotte Mason approach — living books, narration, short lessons, nature study. 6-year history cycle covering ancient through modern. One teacher guide works for whole family grades 1-12 with book suggestions per form/level. Christian perspective but gentle integration. Budget-friendly since many books can be sourced from libraries. Pairs well with SCM's other subjects (geography, nature study, Bible). Best for families specifically seeking Charlotte Mason methodology.

name: Moving Beyond the Page Social Studies
description: Secular, literature-based social studies integrating language arts through thematic units with hands-on projects and critical thinking activities.
notes: Standards-based secular curriculum that goes beyond public school basics. Integrates social studies with language arts through literature. Thematic units with hands-on projects and critical thinking emphasis. Available for ages 7-14 in online or print format. Strong fit for project-based and gifted learners. Less well-known but highly regarded by secular homeschool community.
